observations.cue: sunken rectangular courtyards surrounded by below-grade rooms and a heavy perimeter rim
observations.note: The central open-to-sky void and surrounding earth mass are the load-bearing Yaodong cues; the useful rule is not the copied room coordinates.

observations.cue: each house is authored as a terrain-buried piece rather than a conventional above-grade shell
observations.note: Every building pool entry declares terrain_adaptation=bury, while the village start uses surface projection and beard_thin terrain adaptation.

observations.cue: five related house pieces vary between compact square courts and a larger multi-arm household
observations.note: The roster spans 20-24 blocks wide, 20-33 blocks deep, and 17 blocks high, so the candidate is a settlement family rather than one hero prefab.

observations.cue: earth, mud-brick, stone-brick, planted roof, and timber furnishing layers distinguish the occupied void from the surrounding soil
observations.note: The source is a badlands-oriented brick-vault interpretation, not evidence for a canonical exposed-yellow-earth Yaodong. Its material contrast may calibrate shelter readability, but its exact palette is not a project requirement.

observations.cue: terrain-matching streets, a central well, and small decor pools connect buried households into a village fabric
observations.evidence_pointer: upstream commit 3da53915cd7658b5085f5926ff0205ebd30ee9d9, data/mcs/worldgen/template_pool/village/grotto/center.json, streets.json, decor.json, and well_bottom.json
observations.note: The source value extends beyond a single structure: access, drainage, and shared open space must work at settlement scale.

observations.cue: the source NBTs mix geometry with jigsaw connectors, loot, inventories, block-entity payloads, and optional entities
observations.note: This coupling makes a raw prefab copy unsafe even though every palette block is in the minecraft namespace.

direct_component.cue: yaodong_1.nbt as the lowest-risk bounded prefab candidate
direct_component.rationale: The piece has no entities and is visually self-contained, but it still carries seven jigsaws, 31 block-entity payloads, 19 inventory payloads, six loot-table payloads, source DataVersion 3953, and upstream gameplay semantics. It is suitable only for an isolated sanitize-for-preview feasibility pass.
direct_component.downstream_route: future external-structure-quarantine OpenSpec capability and dedicated CRAFT pipeline; keep raw and sanitized binaries under ignored out/ only, require static audit, deterministic transformation ledger, paired previews, and owner feasibility verdict before any later shipping proposal
direct_component.review_needed: Owner must decide whether the cleaned geometry is valuable enough to justify a separate import/shipping change. This pending decision is not permission to place the NBT under src/main/resources.

direct_component.cue: the complete upstream Grotto village pools as a directly imported settlement
direct_component.rationale: REJECTED for this pass. The village pool includes buildings, streets, decor, wells, jigsaw namespace dependencies, entities, and loot behavior; importing it would be a worldgen and gameplay transplant rather than a bounded building study.
direct_component.downstream_route: no_direct_component: retain the complete village only as inspection evidence; any future Yaodong settlement must enter through a separate region, settlement-group, worldgen, attribution, and gameplay design change
direct_component.review_needed: Do not reconsider the whole-village copy until the single-piece quarantine policy, DataVersion migration path, and owner prefab policy are resolved.

atomic_component.cue: sunken-court retaining rim with a descending entry throat
atomic_component.rationale: The rim, stair/descent, and courtyard threshold form a bounded original operation that can establish the above-grade-to-below-grade transition without copying the source footprint.
atomic_component.downstream_route: form-registry + future yaodong-earth-shelter capability: add a sunken-court rim/descent operation with walkability and head-clearance validation
atomic_component.review_needed: Section and in-game review must confirm the descent reads as an inhabited entrance rather than a mine shaft or defensive pit.

atomic_component.cue: earth-shell room cell opening onto the sunken court
atomic_component.rationale: A repeatable room bay can be authored from an original arch/threshold, earth roof, and interior-depth grammar while varying width and occupation.
atomic_component.downstream_route: building-generation + form-registry: define an earth-shelter room-bay motif reusable around one or more sunken courts
atomic_component.review_needed: Check arch proportions, daylight depth, ceiling thickness, and whether the room remains legible from the courtyard.

generative_grammar.cue: terrain-embedded building slot with occupied floor below the surrounding surface
generative_grammar.rationale: The essential planner rule is to reserve an excavation volume, set courtyard and room floors below grade, preserve an earth roof at terrain level, and connect the entrance back to a walkable surface route.
generative_grammar.downstream_route: future yaodong-earth-shelter capability + building-generation + validation: define surface_y, courtyard_depth, earth_cover, retaining_thickness, entrance_rise, drainage, and excavation-clearance invariants
generative_grammar.review_needed: Terrain integration must be tested on flat and sloped Loess-like sites; exposed cut faces, flooded courts, floating roofs, and blocked stairs must fail.

generative_grammar.cue: cluster grammar with terrain-matching streets, central well, decor nodes, and buried houses
generative_grammar.rationale: A Yaodong settlement needs surface paths that reveal and connect otherwise low-silhouette homes; the shared court/well/street system carries more identity than an isolated buried box.
generative_grammar.downstream_route: settlement-group + town-plan + town-realization + region-profile: add a future northern/Loess settlement fabric only after a single earth-shelter piece is accepted
generative_grammar.review_needed: Plan review must prove that entrances remain discoverable, routes do not cross excavation voids, and the settlement reads as inhabited from surface level.

generative_grammar.cue: compact, medium, and expanded household variants share one buried-court language
generative_grammar.rationale: The five source pieces suggest variation by court size, room-arm count, occupation, and entry position rather than unrelated silhouettes.
generative_grammar.downstream_route: future yaodong-earth-shelter + settlement-group: define scale tiers and household-role parameters with deterministic variant selection
generative_grammar.review_needed: Compare at least three generated tiers; reject variants that lose the sunken court or become ordinary flat-roof houses.

calibration_only.cue: inhabited earth dwelling rather than bunker, quarry, or open pit
calibration_only.visual_question: Do the courtyard, entrances, windows, furnishings, and roof edge make habitation obvious from both surface and courtyard viewpoints?
calibration_only.review_needed: Use low surface, courtyard-eye, and section views; the current top-down isometric preview alone is insufficient.

calibration_only.visual_question: Does the structure merge into surrounding terrain without a rectangular soil pedestal, exposed void edge, or pasted-on green lid?
calibration_only.review_needed: Review against at least one flat and one sloped terrain placement; offline isolated NBT previews cannot settle this question.

calibration_only.visual_question: Can a player immediately find the descent and understand the courtyard-to-room circulation, or does the mass read as a closed square hole?
calibration_only.review_needed: Inspect the interactive layer viewer and later perform an in-game walkability pass before accepting any Yaodong implementation.

calibration_only.visual_question: Can a future palette distinguish a northern earth-shelter settlement without mistaking this source's mud/stone brick vaults for the only authentic Yaodong material language?
calibration_only.review_needed: Calibrate palette against broader architectural references after geometry is legible; do not treat this source block list as a canonical Yaodong palette specification.
